claim_text: Transaction Import & Approval System
A dual-role system that enables Importers to upload and review transaction files, and Approvers to review, approve/reject, and export transactions.
source_quote: Importers to upload and review transaction files

claim_text: file-driven ingestion via File Logs
transaction lifecycle review and approval
role-based interaction constraints
search and filter capability over transactions and file logs
CSV export of filtered transaction sets
file summary view with per-status counts
a transaction-management backend exposing file ingestion, transaction CRUD, and approve/reject operations
a binary blob storage tier holding uploaded source files retrievable by file log id
source_quote: Downloads a file from the server as a stream

claim_text: users have an existing account in the identity provider with at least one assigned role
frontend SPA and backend share an eTLD+1 so SameSite=Strict session cookies are delivered
File Log
A record representing an uploaded file and its processing state, anchoring all transactions extracted from that file.
source_quote: Represents an uploaded file and its processing state

claim_text: Transaction
An individual financial record extracted from a File Log, carrying a reference, date, account, amount, currency, status, and optional user note.
source_quote: Represents individual records extracted from a file

claim_text: A person authenticated to the system who acts in one or more roles.
A named configuration that governs how an uploaded file is interpreted.
A computed view over a single File Log showing total record count and counts per Transaction status.
A non-persistent business judgement that determines whether a Transaction transitions to Approved or to Rejected with a mandatory note.
User uploads File Log
User approves Transaction
User rejects Transaction
File Setting classifies File Log
File Log, Transaction, File Summary
Uploaded → Processing → Completed → Failed
Imported → Approved; Imported → Rejected
A Transaction cannot be approved or rejected unless its current Status is Imported.
A Rejected Transaction must carry a non-empty user note recorded at the moment of rejection.
server begins extracting transactions from the uploaded file
the file has been received by the backend
the File Log row shows status Completed and the file summary becomes available
extraction encountered an unrecoverable error
the File Log row shows status Failed and a retry-validation action becomes available
Approver invokes the approve action on the transaction
the transaction's status badge updates to Approved and the approve/reject actions disappear from the row
Approver invokes the reject action and supplies a user note
the transaction's status badge updates to Rejected, the user note is captured on the transaction, and the approve/reject actions disappear from the row
Importer
quick visual confirmation that an upload succeeded, fast review of file content, clear search across previously uploaded files
Approver
accountable for the correctness of every transaction posted; their approval is the gate before downstream processing
clear visibility into which transactions are awaiting decision, the ability to drill into supporting information, fast bulk filter and export
Successfully import transaction files for downstream approval
Maintain control over which transactions are approved and which are rejected with reason
Provide visibility into file processing status and per-file outcomes
file status is always visible; per-status counts are derivable from the file summary view
Export decided transactions for downstream processing and reconciliation
the export reflects the user's active filter set; the exported file is in CSV format by default
Successfully transmit a chosen file to the system along with its file setting context so the system can create the corresponding File Log.

Given the Importer has selected a file and supplied FileSettingId, FileSettingName, and FileName, when they submit, then a File Log is created and the upload screen surfaces either a success confirmation or a failure message naming the cause.
Inspect the table of uploaded files, their record counts, process dates, and current statuses.

Given the Importer has been authenticated, when they open the file log overview, then they see a list of File Logs with File Name, Process Date, Record Count, and Status, and selecting a row drills into the contained transactions.
Narrow the visible set of file logs and transactions to those matching status, file, date range, amount range, or free-text reference / account input.
Read the derived summary for a single File Log showing total records and counts by transaction status.
Given the Importer has selected a File Log, when they open its file summary, then they see Total records, Imported count, Approved count, and Rejected count.
Inspect the working list of transactions and identify those whose status is still Imported.

Given the Approver has been authenticated, when they open the transactions screen, then they see a list of Transactions with Reference, Date, Account, Amount, Currency, and Status, with row-level approve and reject actions available where the status is Imported.
Move a specific Imported transaction to Approved status with a confirmed deliberate action.
Move a specific Imported transaction to Rejected status and persist the supplied user note.

Given the Approver has selected a transaction with Status = Imported, when they invoke the reject action and supply a mandatory user note, then the transaction's Status transitions to Rejected, the user note is persisted on the transaction, and the action submission cannot complete without a non-empty note.
Given the Approver has applied a filter set to the transactions list, when they invoke export, then a CSV file is produced containing exactly the visible filtered rows and the default format is CSV.
source_quote: Build logic to export the transactions datagrid into csv file

claim_text: the user opens the application and is not yet authenticated
user enters email and password; on submit, success routes to role-specific landing; on failure the form displays a generic credentials-incorrect error message
source_quote: On success → route to role-specific landing

claim_text: invalid credentials → generic incorrect-credentials banner → re-enter credentials
the landing page differs by role: Importer lands on the Dashboard with the upload affordance available; Approver lands on the Dashboard without the upload affordance
the Importer chooses to upload a transaction file
Importer selects the file via the upload affordance; supplies FileSettingId, FileSettingName, and FileName; submits the upload; the system creates a File Log
upload success vs upload failure
only Importers see the upload affordance; Approvers do not
the user opens the Dashboard
user opens the file log overview; the system shows a list of uploaded File Logs with File Name, Process Date, Record Count, and Status
which File Log to drill into
a File Log is in Failed state → row visually marks the failure and offers a retry-validation action → user retries validation or cancels the file
the user opens the Transactions screen
user opens the transaction table; the system shows a list with Reference, Date, Account, Amount, Currency, and Status
which transaction to action; approve vs reject
row-level approve and reject actions are visible only to Approvers; Importers see the same list without those actions
the Approver selects a Transaction with Status = Imported and chooses approve
Approver chooses approve; confirmation appears; on confirm the transaction's Status transitions to Approved
the Approver selects a Transaction with Status = Imported and chooses reject
Approver chooses reject; reject form appears requiring a mandatory user note; on submit with non-empty note the transaction's Status transitions to Rejected and the user note is persisted
submit with note vs cancel; note empty vs non-empty
the Approver chooses to export the currently filtered transaction set
Approver applies a filter set; invokes export; the system produces a CSV file containing exactly the visible filtered rows
only Approvers see the export action
the user opens the file summary for a specific File Log
both roles see the summary
the user opens the transactions screen or the file logs screen
The system authenticates a user via email and password and returns a session indicator.
On success the user is routed to a role-specific landing; on failure a generic credentials-incorrect message is shown.
source_quote: On failure, returns 401 with a deliberately generic message

claim_text: The system signs the user out and invalidates the active session.
source_quote: Sign out and invalidate the session cookie

claim_text: The system returns the authenticated user's profile (identity and roles).
The authenticated user's identity and assigned roles are returned and rendered in the application chrome.
The system lists File Logs, filterable by IsActive.
source_quote: Gets a list of file log entries

claim_text: The system retrieves per-File-Log process log entries for a specific LogId.
source_quote: Gets a list of file process log entries for a file log entry

claim_text: The system supports downloading the parsed data file for a File Log by LogId.
source_quote: Download a data file with input ID

claim_text: The system supports downloading the original uploaded file for a File Log by FileLogId.
source_quote: Download a file with input ID

claim_text: The system supports downloading a bulk-error file for a File Log when one exists.
source_quote: Download a bulk error file with input ID

claim_text: The bulk-error download is only offered when an error file exists.
The system supports cancelling and deactivating a File Log by LogId.
The File Log is deactivated and removed from the active list.
source_quote: Deactivate and delete a file from the staging table

claim_text: The system returns the column definitions for the validation-error view of a File Log.
source_quote: Get stadium column definitions for a specific table

claim_text: The UI renders the columns named by the system for that file.
source_quote: Returns UI column definitions for the requested table

claim_text: The system returns the per-row validation errors for a File Log as a single JSON array string.
source_quote: Get invalid rows for a specific file as a single JSON array

claim_text: The UI renders the listed error rows.
The system lists Transactions with Reference, Date, Account, Amount, Currency, Status, and UserNote.
source_quote: Get a list of all transactions

claim_text: The system approves a Transaction by Id, transitioning its Status to Approved.
source_quote: Sets the Status of the transaction to 'Approved'

claim_text: The system rejects a Transaction by Id with a captured user note, transitioning its Status to Rejected.
source_quote: Sets the Status of the transaction to 'Rejected' and records the supplied user note

claim_text: The system lists, creates, updates, and deletes Users (administrative scope).
source_quote: Get a list of all users

claim_text: The system lists, creates, updates, and deletes Roles, including Role-to-Page assignment.
source_quote: Get a list of all roles

claim_text: The system lists Pages available for assignment to Roles.
source_quote: Get a list of all pages

claim_text: Username and password are required.
An associated File Setting must be selected.
A non-empty user note is required when rejecting.
Currency must be one of the configured ISO-4217 codes (e.g. ZAR).
The upload surface accepts a single file at a time; the chosen file's name is shown back to the user; the user is informed of upload progress and outcome.
The summary view is accessible from a File Log and shows the four counts; counts update as transactions are decided.
Status changes on a transaction reflect immediately on the actioned transaction in the list.
none in the initial scope — credentials-only login per the provided authentication contract.
Status, UserNote, LastChangedUser, LastChangedDate
LastChangedUser, LastChangedDate, CurrentStatus, LastExecutedActivityName
